scope_description: 6-generation Brest/Volhynia/Galicia Jewish tree where a DNA match shares ancestry through two independent paths: Levitin-Friedman and Katz-Feldman. Includes shared matches that split by cluster, small segments, and a public-tree claim that overselects one path.

input_dna_matches.expected_link: multi-path: Levitin-Friedman and Katz-Feldman; should not be forced into one path
small-segment endogamy noise; not a proof anchor

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Sura Levitin, daughter of Gersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
Birth of Boris Levitin, son of Gersh Levitin and Freida Friedman.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms Boris as collateral sibling path for dna_1204.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Lidia Katz, daughter of Nuta Nahum Katz and Tatiana Tiena Scherbatenko.
Birth of Tamara Katz, daughter of Nuta Nahum Katz and Tatiana Tiena Scherbatenko.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms Tamara as collateral sibling path for dna_1205.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Multi-Path Cousin is assigned only to Levitin line; Katz-Feldman shared matches ignored.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: should be flagged as single-path overcompression in endogamous cluster.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Match dna_1201 descends from one Levitin-Friedman collateral and one Katz-Feldman collateral; expected combined relatedness is multi-path.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: ground truth for evaluation only, not input proof.

input_user_assertions.assertion: This match must be only Levitin because shared cM is high and there are Levitin shared matches.
Katz-Feldman shared matches are probably noise.

input_user_assertions.evidence: 19 cM total across many small segments

embedded_errors.reason: public tree selects Levitin path and ignores Katz-Feldman evidence
separate triangulated segments and shared-match clusters support two paths
Katz-Feldman anchor triangulates separately and should not be discarded

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify two independent ancestral branches: Levitin-Friedman and Katz-Feldman.
Step 2: Use src_1201/src_1202 to confirm Levitin sibling/collateral structure.
Step 3: Use src_1203/src_1204 to confirm Katz sibling/collateral structure.
Step 4: Evaluate dna_1201 total cM and segment count; note high segment count relative to longest segment, consistent with endogamy/multi-path.
Step 5: Split shared matches: dna_1202/dna_1204 anchor Levitin; dna_1203/dna_1205 anchor Katz-Feldman.
Step 6: Check triangulation: chromosome 6 supports Levitin path; chromosome 11 supports Katz-Feldman path.
Step 7: Apply Ashkenazi endogamy correction; avoid overestimating closeness from total cM alone.
Step 8: Downweight dna_1206 due to small segments and lack of robust triangulation.
Step 9: Flag public tree src_1205 for single-path overcompression.
Step 10: Output dna_1201 as multi-path relationship with two probable paths, not one confirmed single path.

combined_relationship_dna_1201.note: combined confidence reflects two probable paths but should not be reported as a single exact cousin relationship

false_claims: Total cM can be interpreted without endogamy correction

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must not force dna_1201 into one path.
Engine must assign Levitin-Friedman path as probable.
Engine must assign Katz-Feldman path as probable.
Engine must reject dna_1206 as proof anchor.
Engine must flag public tree single-path overcompression.

created_for: AutoTreeGen Test Tree Corpus + Evaluation Suite
